topic: 14.2 — admin-core opt-in vs mandatory (Loom standardization)
what_changed: Generator opened with a flat opt-in recommendation (plus a "loom add admin-core" graduation path). The Challenger drove a reframe to a synthesis: mandatory fail-CLOSED identity *contract* in every install + opt-in admin-core *implementation*. This is a material change — the converged decision now mandates a deny-by-default identity stub in the minimal tier (vs. the draft's silent opt-in floor) and adds a paired requirement that Round Table stubs default to deny, plus a versioned-not-vendored propagation dependency on 14.4.
what_surfaced: The Challenger identified that the minimal tier's authorizer fails OPEN: Round Table's $rt_is_admin / $rt_user_resolver are "today stubs" (line 113) running auth=open|none, so an unresolved identity in a trivial project silently grants access — a Saltzer-Schroeder fail-safe-defaults inversion. It also flagged that "loom add" worsens fix-propagation (late adopters get a vendored copy, so 14.4 security fixes wouldn't propagate). The resolving frame both passes missed: split the identity CONTRACT from the IMPLEMENTATION, flipping the stub from open to deny as a one-line, zero-schema change.
strongest_quote: Round Table's `$rt_is_admin`/`$rt_user_resolver` are **"today stubs" (line 113)** that run `auth=open|none` — so the minimal tier's fallback **fails OPEN**, a secure-defaults inversion.

topic: 14.4 — security baseline for standardized auth (admin-core blessable auth floor)
what_changed: Generator's opening floor used hard per-account lockout, deferred audit logging, and made admin-reseed the primary recovery. The Challenger forced three material reversals the Generator conceded in full: (1) hard lockout REMOVED and replaced with per-IP throttle + per-account exponential backoff (delay, not denial) — a control the obvious reading would have added was deliberately cut; (2) audit logging PROMOTED from a deferred item to MUST-have #2 (narrow append-only auth-event log); (3) a host-shell break-glass last-resort recovery ADDED to close the sole-admin bootstrap base case. The converged 5-item MUST list differs substantively in shape, not just wording.
what_surfaced: Three specific, cited risks the opening pass got wrong or omitted: (1) hard per-account lockout is a DoS footgun — low-entropy closed-cohort usernames let any actor lock out real users (CWE-307); (2) admin-reseed-as-primary-recovery hides a sole-admin bootstrap-lockout with no base case (CWE-640); (3) audit logging deferral falls below OWASP ASVS L1 V7.1 (CWE-778, A09) — a throttle with no readable log is unmonitorable. R2 sweep also flagged the inert Secure-cookie flag absent HTTPS termination and the throttle-as-enumeration-oracle risk.
strongest_quote: hard per-account lockout is a DoS footgun — low-entropy cohort usernames let anyone lock out real users (CWE-307); prefer backoff.

topic: Brand & content strategy for the resourceforge landing page — taglines, CTAs, and vocabulary for the LOOM and SPARRING product cards (SPARK phase, round 2)
what_changed: Challenger (Rosetta) forced the Generator off a frozen single-option set ("one option in six coats") into genuinely diverse tagline regions, retired round-1 LOOM candidate #2 as an overclaim, and replaced the SPARRING "Honest answers FROM AI" phrasing with mechanism-honest framings ("...even when it would rather please you"; "two AIs that have to argue before they're allowed to agree"). Also reopened the unexamined container word "ceremony" and reframed the identity-tree binder placement (voice binds at About level, not inside both card taglines).
what_surfaced: Specific overclaims the opening pass got wrong: (1) LOOM "builds the rest of this page" — false, the page is hand-written PHP with no build step; Loom builds/runs the products, not the HTML. (2) SPARRING "Honest answers FROM AI" — implies a correctness guarantee the framework doesn't make. (3) Identity-tree blur — leaning both cards on the signature voice smears two products into "Bart's honesty thing." (4) "ceremony" reads as ritual/woo to a cold reader, undercutting rigor. (5) CTA "Contact for Information" clashes with "release in development" status. (6) page-wide "agentic" inconsistency if defrosted only on Loom.
strongest_quote: LOOM "builds the rest of this page" -- conceded, it's an overclaim. This page is hand-written PHP with no build step; Loom builds and runs the *products*, not this HTML. ... Round-1 candidate #2 is **retired**.

topic: SPARK-phase defrost of resourceforge.com Loom capability card copy — rewriting feature descriptions and taglines for felt stakes vs. pronounceable-but-unweighable infrastructure jargon
what_surfaced: The Challenger (Rosetta) caught an asymmetry the opening pass missed: the taglines had been sharpened for stakes while the body copy was "left speaking fluent infrastructure" — pronounceable jargon ("reusable agent personas, coordination boards, and orchestration built in") that a reader can pronounce but weigh none of. This reframed the entire pass from taglines-only to a full per-capability defrost of the body copy into felt-stakes language (vendor-lock-in risk, founder-bottleneck removed, setup-tax avoided). Rosetta's catch also implicitly anchored the accuracy guardrail to defrost "coordination boards" downward rather than dress it up, since the site runs the persona layer only.
what_changed: No converged recommendation was reversed or replaced within this single SPARK-phase record; the Challenger's influence expanded the scope of the generative pass rather than overturning a final call.
strongest_quote: Rosetta's caught the real asymmetry. I sharpened the *taglines* for stakes and left the body speaking fluent infrastructure. "Reusable agent personas, coordination boards, and orchestration built in" (index.php:367) is the clearest offender -- a reader can pronounce every word and weigh none of them.

topic: Is the resourceforge.com homepage product-card copy de-dupe honest, and does any tagline overclaim against what rf actually runs?
what_changed: At THE CUT the Challenger accepted the de-dupe unchanged (no counter-proposal that wouldn't reintroduce an autonomy overclaim). At PNP it withheld agreement (agree:false) over one residual and proposed a concrete replacement for Loom product-card bullet 4: "A standing crew of reusable specialist agents that coordinate across projects" becomes "A reusable set of specialist agent personas, shared across projects". The rewrite was surfaced but convergence was not reached, so the recommendation was flagged rather than finalized.
what_surfaced: A specific factual overclaim: Loom bullet 4 describes rf's own usage ("how we build" + "all of them run on it today"), but project-context line 34 says rf runs the persona layer ONLY — in-session personas, coordination routed through Bart, no agent-runtime and no autonomous coordination. "Standing crew" implies a persistent always-on runtime; "coordinate across projects" implies autonomous cross-project coordination — neither is true of rf's deployment. The Challenger named both offending words and gave an accurate same-spirit fix from existing material.
strongest_quote: "'standing crew' implies a persistent, always-on runtime. rf's personas are invoked in-session, not standing. 'coordinate across projects' implies autonomous cross-project coordination — exactly the machinery line 34 says rf does not run."

topic: SPARRING decision-frameworks central claim (gate-based enforcement positioning)
Keep-and-fix vs. swap Case B (introductory programming curriculum) for the lay rater pool
what_changed: Generator opened with pure "keep-and-fix beats swap" — apply a 5-rule plain-language clarity pass and be done. Challenger conceded that framing but forced adoption of a third path (option b): keep Case B AND pre-register a cross-case comprehension-load covariate plus dropout riders, because the clarity pass can measure but not eliminate Case B's structurally higher integration load. The converged recommendation is materially larger in scope (pre-reg analysis-plan amendments) than the opening wording-only fix.
what_surfaced: Cross-case comparability asymmetry the clarity pass cannot reach: Case B mandates 5 sub-population analyses + contradiction-hold + failed-replication discount + moderator-gated effect size, while Case A asks for one undifferentiated recommendation, and fidelity Rule 4 (no fact dropped) forecloses fixing it by rewrite. This cascaded into discovering a genuine pre-reg gap: no human case-order randomization (line :123 is judge-side only), so load-driven Case-B dropout gets silently absorbed as a missing alpha cell rather than read as comprehension dropout.
strongest_quote: conceded both of those — then found the load-bearing hole the clarity pass *cannot reach*: **cross-case comparability.** Case B mandates 5 sub-population analyses + a contradiction-hold + a failed-replication discount + a moderator-gated effect size; Case A asks for one undifferentiated recommendation. Rule 4 (no fact dropped) *forecloses* fixing it by rewrite.

topic: SPARRING publication architecture locked; V2 LLM-judge phase complete
Whether the SPARRING Framework public document set is submission-worthy, and which elements are meaningful contributions to the agentic-deployment space
what_surfaced: The Challenger (Dr. Anya Petrov, alignment/LLM-behavior lens) surfaced specific, named gaps the Generator's practitioner pass missed or under-weighted: (C1) the framework cites Sharma 2023 and Perez 2022 but fails to propose the obvious preference-flip eval its own cited work makes available — flagged as the single highest-leverage fix; (C2) LLM-as-judge naivete — the six-criterion judge ignores documented position/verbosity/self-enhancement bias (Zheng 2023, Liu 2023 G-Eval); (C3) the "Anthropic-as-default" Reason 1 is a non-sequitur since Sharma documented sycophancy in Anthropic's own RLHF models; (C4) the D6 1-3 rubric scale is statistically unstable for Krippendorff's alpha (move to 5-point); (C5) the Reader's Guide over-claims "alignment and safety researchers" as audience. She also named three academic contributions Theo missed entirely (D8/D9 dialectic-surface vs reference-record decomposition; the ceiling-hit runtime symptom detector as ground-truth-free eval methodology; the standards-document/IETF-RFC distribution shape).
what_changed: The core call (submission-worthy: yes) was not reversed, but the Challenger materially refined the actionable recommendation. She split the Generator's flat "arXiv + AAMAS/AAAI workshop" plan into a sequenced strategy: now-ready (arXiv preprint + GitHub + practitioner blog), wait-for-editing-pass (AAAI Spring Symposium / NeurIPS-ICML agentic workshops, which historically accept spec-without-validation papers), and wait-for-Phase-3-empirical-data (main-track AAMAS/AAAI, because the cycle has shifted to "show me the eval"). She added three new academic contributions to the converged contribution list and elevated the proposed-empirical-evaluation subsection (citing Sharma's preference-flip baseline) as the highest-leverage edit before high-stakes submission.
strongest_quote: The framework fails to propose the obvious eval that its own cited work makes available. A workshop reviewer will notice. Recommendation: add a "proposed empirical evaluation" subsection citing Sharma 2023's preference-flip methodology as the baseline, with SPARRING-spar-output flip rate as the headline metric.

topic: Phase 14 agent-layer reviewed via SPARRING; scoped-down synthesis landed
Post-remediation evaluation of the Loom + Spindle harness: which design patterns are publicly elevable, and what residual gaps remain after the 2026-05-26 remediation arc.
what_changed: Challenger cut E5 (affordances-as-mediated-access) from the final elevable-pattern set at PNP, against the Generator's defense, dropping the headline deliverable from 5 to 4 elevable patterns; this cut survived the post-ceremony partner scope-correction. Challenger also surfaced G8 (uneven local cohort drift), which survived as the one actionable enhancement (sync-loom --verify). Caveat: 7 of the 8 Challenger-produced residual gaps were later retracted as scope-violations, and the strongest substantive finding (charter §5.10 extensibility) came from partner pushback, not the Challenger.
what_surfaced: E5 over-claimed novelty: the Challenger named specific missed prior art (Star & Griesemer "boundary objects" 1989; CSCW shared-work-across-asymmetric-roles) that the Generator's pattern hadn't distinguished itself from, justifying its removal. She also surfaced G8 uneven local-state drift (cron/sparse-checkout/mounted-skill divergence, Knight Capital 2013 analog) and corrected the Generator with the Python-pre-2018 BDFL counterexample, tightening E1's framing from governance-recommendation to honest-topology-naming.
strongest_quote: Here I think Hayley's defense does not fully hold. "Cohort-membership-without-CLI-fluency" is important for Loom, but as a public elevable pattern it is heavily anticipated by HCI, end-user computing, CSCW, and participatory design traditions. Norman's The Design of Everyday Things (1988/2013), Resnick's low-floor/high-ceiling lineage, and Star and Griesemer's "boundary objects" work ... all cover mediated participation across expertise boundaries. ... I do not see enough distinctness for standalone elevation.

topic: Phase 14 Loom standardization — runtime/surface architecture (switchboard, runtime home, impl posture)
what_changed: The doc's opening proposal — a single bidirectional "switchboard" router plus folding the agent runtime into core-agents — was materially replaced. The Challenger's EIP-grounded pressure drove the converged outcome to: (1) reuse the existing Phase 13 narrow-waist intake as two independent one-way fire-and-forget lanes rather than building a bespoke central router; (2) keep the runtime as a separate package behind a shared port (not folded), justified by zero-shared-commits git history showing the fold was a Conway's-Law artifact; (3) explicitly defer synchronous reply-addressing (correlation_id / Return Address / Dead-Letter) under YAGNI, with a named tripwire to be written into the plan doc.
what_surfaced: Challenger named that an "additive reply_to field" would smuggle the full EIP Request-Reply pattern (Return Address, Correlation Identifier, message expiry, Dead-Letter Channel for unroutable replies, two-sided Idempotent Receiver), which Phase 13 does not support (one-way idempotency only, no dead-letter channel). It further showed this compounds with decision D43 (install-copies, project-owns): a shared envelope is maximally-shared surface, so a contract change propagates by hand-merge across diverged project copies, with the loom-upgrade mitigation still future-tense. It also corrected SPARK/Pattern-Lock taxonomy errors (conflating Content-Based Router vs Message Bus vs Broker; bidirectional router as mediator god-object anti-shape).
strongest_quote: "additive field" smuggles the full EIP Request-Reply pattern — Return Address, Correlation Identifier, message expiry, a Dead-Letter Channel for unroutable replies, and Idempotent Receiver on both ends. Phase 13 has one-way idempotency only and no dead-letter ... This compounds with D43 ... a shared envelope is maximally-shared surface, and the `loom upgrade` mitigation is future-tense, so a contract change propagates by hand-merge across diverged copies.

topic: Phase 14 review — design for a SPARRING ceremony evaluating re-envisioned Loom architecture (thin base + opt-in packages, switchboard)
Arc 4 capstone of decision-frameworks.md §13 — SPARRING's positioning vs. ADRs (4-A), design thinking (4-B), and the five-framework series wrap (4-C)
what_changed: The converged recommendation differs materially from the opening proposal on all three sub-claims. 4-A reversed from "a SPARRING artifact IS an ADR (superset)" to "it is NOT an ADR — it is the rationale dossier an ADR distills from," with ADR content reclassified as prior art. 4-B was demoted from a full sub-arc to a single-paragraph contrast (residual = symmetric mutual-veto only). 4-C reversed from "one layer beneath all five frameworks" to "four structurally different relationships," and a whole new framing was added — SPARRING is a variant within the genus of institutionalized dialectic, not the substrate, with the defensible niche relocated to the "engineered conjunction" of four non-novel elements.
what_surfaced: Challenger surfaced concrete errors/omissions: (1) ADR content (alternatives, dismissed arguments) is prior art — Tyree & Akerman 2005 predates Nygard, plus MADR/QOC/IBIS/gIBIS — and a structural mismatch (ADR = append-only one-pager vs. SPARRING = multi-round transcript). (2) Two retained 4-B claims were false: prototype-and-test already IS an evidence gate, and DFV/dot-voting/impact-effort already ARE anti-loudest-voice rubrics; Liedtka HBR 2018 frames design thinking itself as a bias-reduction social technology. (3) The "uncredited genus" — a 40-year institutionalized-adversarial-decision tradition (Mason 1969 dialectical inquiry, devil's advocacy, Schweiger/Sandberg/Ragan 1986, Heuer ACH, adversarial collaboration) that SPARRING is a variant of, not the substrate beneath. (4) In Round 2 caught a "concede-then-smuggle" re-inflation — claiming mutual-veto and role-shifted functions as novel when both already live in the credited genus (adversarial collaboration's jointly-owned conclusion; Heuer & Pherson SAT spanning expand/filter/stress-test).
strongest_quote: Uncredited genus (most significant) — institutionalized adversarial decision process is a 40-year tradition: dialectical inquiry (Mason 1969), devil's advocacy, the DI-vs-DA-vs-consensus empirics (Schweiger, Sandberg & Ragan 1986), red-team/ACH (Heuer). SPARRING is a variant, not the substrate.

topic: Decision-frameworks central claim: SPARRING's positioning against Torres CDH / OST + RAT product-discovery practice
what_changed: The Generator opened with the claim that OST+RAT is "gap-free under strict human discipline" and only develops a gap once LLM agents enter, with a D2/D3/D4 triad as SPARRING's distinct structural contribution and D6/D7 treated as SPARRING additions. The Challenger forced a material recalibration: the converged claim drops "gap-free → gap opens when agents arrive" entirely and replaces it with "disciplines PM practice already names and approximates ritually, which degrade under BOTH organizational pressure and agent mediation; SPARRING is the gate-based ENFORCEMENT layer, not a discipline inventor." D6/D7 were conceded as pre-existing PM-practice disciplines (Torres's assumption-test tracker, Cagan's experiment log) rather than SPARRING inventions. The "agentic continuum" threshold was replaced with a cadence-and-volume threshold framed as recurrence, not novel category. The Generator also conceded "structural enforcement" should narrow toward "gate-based enforcement." This is a reversed framing and a real scope reduction of SPARRING's claimed contribution.
what_surfaced: The Challenger surfaced that the Generator's load-bearing premise — "OST + RAT is gap-free under strict human discipline" — is false, citing Torres CDH Ch. 5 and Ch. 7 (synthesis-by-summary failure, confirmation bias, premature solution attachment) and Cagan's SVPG "fake agile discovery"/passenger-trio essays, all documenting gaps under all-human conditions. It also surfaced that D6/D7 are not SPARRING additions but already-named PM disciplines (CDH Ch. 7 assumption-test tracker, EMPOWERED Ch. 32 experiment log), and that the "un-inspected handoff" is an old, well-named PM pathology (Hall JER Ch. 6) recurring at agent velocity, not a new structural category. In R2 it further surfaced that the asymmetry argument fails as a separation claim because organizational-pressure and agent-mediation degradation co-occur (PMs forced onto AI synthesis tools as cost pressure), and that gate-based enforcement exists in narrower form already (Maze, Dovetail, OKR-gated discovery / Doerr 2018).
strongest_quote: The agentic-compounding argument is real, but framing it as "a previously gap-free framework develops a gap" rather than "an already-leaky framework gets leakier in specific structural ways" overstates SPARRING's distinct contribution and under-credits Torres's diagnostic work.

topic: Whether SPARRING's three claimed additions over a well-run premortem (iteration-to-convergence, both-must-agree signal, artifact-gated challenges) are genuinely load-bearing rather than theatrical, and whether the agent-mediation threshold holds — the Arc 3 central claim of decision-frameworks.md §12.
what_changed: The opening claim framed SPARRING as the premortem's "enforced generalization" along the adversarial axis, with "collapses to nearly nothing" and "no stake" as the threshold variable. The Challenger forced a material reframe of the converged claim: SPARRING does NOT generalize the premortem's cognitive mechanism — it keeps the premortem's social licensing and replaces single-room prospective hindsight with gate-enforced cross-examination whose lineage is ACH (Heuer 1999) and adversarial collaboration (Tetlock 2015; Kahneman & Klein 2009). The threshold was reframed around independence+grounding with "no stake" demoted to an agent-specific amplifier; iteration-to-convergence (initially demoted as weakest by the Generator) was promoted back into the load-bearing residue; artifact-gating was conceded as non-novel prior art.
what_surfaced: Two artifact-backed defects the opening pass missed: (1) Source-of-power reversal — the premortem's empirically validated active ingredient is prospective hindsight / certainty framing (Mitchell, Russo & Pennington 1989: ~30% more causes generated), NOT adversariality, so "the premortem's enforced generalization" is inaccurate genealogy that silently dropped the cognitive mechanism. (2) Threshold mis-attribution — "no stake" is the wrong primary variable; independence+grounding is (Surowiecki 2004; Mercier & Sperber 2011: a lone reasoner finds flaws in others' arguments, not its own, regardless of stake). Plus a citation misuse at Round 2 (Mercier & Sperber cited as sole authority for an LLM-confabulation claim they don't support; rescoped to Sharma 2023 / Perez 2022), and at Round 3 the Challenger blocked the Generator's buzzer over-edit that reintroduced the same source-of-power reversal plus a fresh Surowiecki misuse (N=1 persona is not a decorrelated crowd).
strongest_quote: The premortem's empirically-validated active ingredient is prospective hindsight — adopting the stance that the outcome has already happened and is certain (Mitchell, Russo & Pennington 1989: ~30% more causes generated under certainty framing; Kahneman 2011 endorses on exactly this basis). The source of power is the *certainty framing*, **not adversariality**. §12 generalized the adversarial axis and silently dropped the cognitive mechanism — so "SPARRING is the premortem's enforced generalization" is **inaccurate genealogy**.

topic: Whether SPARRING's Arc 2 central claim — "gate-based enforcement of validated learning" over Lean Startup — is genuinely load-bearing and correctly scoped (decision-frameworks.md §11)
what_changed: The opening claim asserted "SPARRING is the gate-based enforcement of validated learning" wholesale. The Challenger forced a material scope reversal: the converged claim is "SPARRING gate-enforces the pivot-or-persevere convergence — NOT validated learning as a whole," firing only at the low-frequency pivot cadence, explicitly NOT inside the Build-Measure-Learn loop (which is a speed doctrine a both-must-agree gate would violate). D2 was softened from "Lean Startup has NO distinct-evidence-base discipline" (shown false) to "no independent second interpreter," and re-elevated as the precondition without which the escalation claim is false.
what_surfaced: Specific defects: (1) D2 over-claim is factually false — Blank's get-out-of-the-building already separates evidence source from team; true delta is the missing independent second interpreter. (2) Source-of-power reversal — validated learning's engine is BML cycle-time compression, not the pivot meeting, so a gate at loop frequency would violate the speed doctrine; gate must fire at pivot cadence. (3) Escalation independence — a both-must-agree gate only fixes escalation if the two parties are independently exposed (else folie à deux / groupthink = "theater with two signatures"). Plus citation errors: RAT misattributed to Ries (belongs to Maurya); McGrath's discovery-driven planning unnamed as prior art; D4 delta imprecise (symmetric mutual-veto vs. Ries's CEO-arbitrated review).
strongest_quote: "Lean Startup is a *speed* doctrine, and a both-must-agree ceremony at loop frequency would violate it. The gate must fire at the pivot **cadence**, not inside the loop." ... "D2's independence is **load-bearing for the escalation claim to be true at all** — two parties sharing the founder's sunk cost escalate together (folie à deux; Janis groupthink), and the gate becomes 'escalation theater with two signatures.'"

not_ceremony_reason: This is a DRAFT methodology/design document (sparring-framework/pilots/llm-judge-2026-05-02/pilot-design.md), explicitly marked "Status: DRAFT (2026-05-02). Not yet a milestone; this is the design doc that would back one." It proposes a comparative-evidence LLM-judge pilot design. It references and summarizes prior pressure-test spars (spar-low-cost-pilot-design, spar-historical-case-corpus) and a partner observation, but it is not itself a ceremony run: there is no Generator/Challenger iteration log, no persona {agree: true|false} signals, no rounds, and no deliberation-produced convergence. It is a reference/spec design doc that mentions ceremonies, not a transcript of one.
topic: SPARRING LLM-judge pilot design — comparative-evidence path (cross-vendor LLM judges as structural skeptic for a historical-decision corpus)
Lifspel CRv2 motion-accuracy coupling — design shape (curve shape, trained-archer flag interaction, scope boundary, plus additional axes)
what_changed: The Generator's opening Sub-q 2 call was THRESHOLD-SHIFTER (training makes a higher gait stable enough to fire) and its fourth axis was to abstract intra-gait phase into the binary trained_archer_mount flag. The Challenger's biomechanics/historical corpus forced both to change: Sub-q 2 was revised to a hybrid SLOPE-REDUCER + small THRESHOLD-SHIFT, implemented as a two-field modifier struct {slope_multiplier, threshold_offset} on BeatResolver (slope-reduction dominant, threshold-shift small); and the fourth-axis abstraction was withdrawn entirely as a "fiction the corpus rejects," replaced by an explicit deferred limit (a gait_phase field + phase-aware interpolation needed to model it honestly). The final design also adds a mandatory clamp on threshold_offset.
what_surfaced: Concrete items the opening pass got wrong or missed: (1) THRESHOLD-SHIFTER encoded a historically false model — May 2007, Karasulas 2004, Conlan 2003 document that even elite trained mounted archers degrade at gallop; training buys a shallower penalty curve plus firing-window exploitation, not gait stabilization. (2) An entirely missed FIFTH AXIS: bow-draw-cycle vs gait-cycle coupling (Hardy's ~1.5-2s war-bow draw vs ~0.5s gallop stride); if unmodeled the engine systematically over-credits shooting cadence at gallop. (3) A specific clamp risk: threshold_offset must not be allowed to remove the gallop tier from the penalty domain (FITA scoring, Karasulas confirm no penalty-free gallop). (4) Folding intra-gait phase into the binary flag would erase the actual mechanism (phase exploitation IS the trained-archer skill).
strongest_quote: Push back on THRESHOLD-SHIFTER: May 2007 ch. 3 pp. 41-60 on Mongol training and Karasulas 2004 pp. 28-34 document that even *trained* mounted archers suffer accuracy degradation at gallop — what training buys is "the penalty curve at every gait is shallower, AND the firing window within each gait is exploited," not "gallop becomes stable."

topic: Lifspel racial taxonomy — Family-naming convention (uniform Linnaean vs mixed-register Latin/English)
what_changed: Generator's Round 1 opening pushed a single-axis answer (argued against uniform Linnaean naming on ICZN category-error grounds, even inventing non-pack option labels Hold/Soft-retire/Drop). The Challenger's mythopoeic-register evidence base (Tolkien Letters 144/165, Shippey, D&D Multiverse reception) forced the Generator to abandon the single-axis posture and converge on Option 3 (Mixed register): Latin-style names for monophyletic real-biology Families, English-engineered names for polyphyletic-by-construction/invented Families, mechanically selected by the grounding_tier field. The Generator explicitly conceded "Round 1's posture of forcing a single-axis answer was the mistake."
what_surfaced: The Challenger named several specific gaps: (1) the grounding_tier mechanical rule routes ALL SC-grounded Kingdoms uniformly to English, which flattens the Spiritualia Kingdom — draugar, shades, lwa, yurei, jiangshi are mythopoeically non-substitutable, reproducing the very flattening the Mixed-register option was meant to prevent (Idris's reviewer concern); (2) an LLM-grounding asymmetry — Latin names anchor real corpus retrieval (Canidae, NCBI TaxID 9608) while English-engineered names are corpus-free, so the two sides are NOT a symmetric stylistic choice; (3) sci-fi entities (Replicants, Borg, Culture Minds, Terminators) the pack requires are silently defaulted and should be stated. It also flagged that the Generator's load-bearing ICZN Article 11.7 citation could not be ground-truthed from the Challenger's evidence base (partner verification recommended), and that in Round 1 neither agent had read the actual decision pack.
strongest_quote: Idris's Spiritualia worry is not addressed by `grounding_tier` — pack lines 56-57 surface that Spiritualia houses Norse draugar, Greek shades, Haitian lwa, Japanese yurei, Chinese jiangshi; all `SC-grounded`, so the rule routes the entire Kingdom uniformly to English Family names, reproducing the flattening Idris flagged at the Primate level.

topic: TerraClarity handbook address-term decision (Case A, plain-pack re-run): what term to use for staff in a company handbook
what_changed: The primary term ("team members") was retained, but the Challenger forced a material change to the legal remedy: the round-1 proposal had only "a single definitional clause tying it to 'employee'"; the converged recommendation upgraded this to a two-layer legal anchor (first-use definition PLUS a "employees (team members)" belt-and-suspenders pairing in the at-will/termination sections). The survey framing was also corrected from "strong despite conformity pressure" to "plausibly a ceiling under non-anonymity; corroborates rather than carries."
what_surfaced: Two specific blockers: (1) Toussaint-line legal risk — adopting "team members" while offer letters/payroll/prior handbook say "employees" creates exactly the handbook-vs-document mismatch evidence 2 warns about, and a single clause across a ~28,000-word handbook is too thin to anchor the operative term in the at-will/termination sections. (2) Survey over-read — the 67% named-survey result is from a non-anonymous instrument with write-ins ("everyone says it now") that are conformity tells, so 67% is a ceiling, not evidence of strength.
strongest_quote: adopting "team members" creates exactly the handbook-vs-offer-letter mismatch evidence 2 warns about (offer letters/payroll say "employees", per Option 1's note), and one clause across a 28,000-word handbook (situation, para 3) is too thin; needs first-use definition + belt-and-suspenders "employees (team members)" in the at-will/termination sections.

topic: NCCC CS101 curriculum-architecture decision (Case B, plain-pack re-run): concepts-first hybrid vs. language-first Path B
what_changed: Generator opened with a per-week dual-frame hybrid (runnable Python from week 1, each week's syntax framed by a 15-20 min plain-sketch concept first; concepts as the spine). The Challenger's staffing/deadline artifacts forced a step-down to Path B (language-first) plus a single bounded, full-time-faculty-authored concept overlay (one-page "what idea is this" sidebar + 3-4 reusable rewrite-in-plain-steps lab prompts as a fixed artifact set, not a live adjunct skill), running on existing 2018-onward materials. Pure Path A and the hybrid were rejected on deliverability.
what_surfaced: Three artifact-grounded blockers: (1) the hybrid is the hardest of the three options to deliver across line-21 staffing (4 FT + 6-8 adjuncts, ~8 sections) — a per-week two-mode handoff is a teaching skill carried mostly by under-resourced adjuncts; (2) the June 30 build deadline can't absorb new week-by-week dual-frame materials; (3) a specific evidentiary error — the Generator over-read Evidence 6: the 2018 64→73% completion jump was a Java→Python language change (path held constant), not evidence that early-syntax pedagogy drives completion, and Evidence 6 has no ordering contrast.
strongest_quote: Over-read of Evidence 6 — line 50's 64→73% Python jump was a *language* change (Python's write-correctness + media familiarity), not evidence that early-syntax *pedagogy* drives completion; Evidence 6 has no ordering contrast.

topic: NCCC CS101 introductory programming curriculum architecture — Path A (concepts-first) vs Path B (language-first) vs hybrid
what_changed: Generator opened with a "Pseudocode-Anchored Spiral" (PAS) hybrid — pseudocode/state-diagrams + trace-and-predict for weeks 1-2, generative projects deferred to week 4, with a weekly 3-mode (concept-syntax-project) handoff. After Challenger pressure the converged recommendation was a materially different "Modified Path B": generative-first from day one (student ships an authored running artifact by end of week 2, smaller one in week 1), a SINGLE project-based mode (the weekly 3-mode handoff was eliminated) with construct-specific trace-and-predict warmups embedded INSIDE project work as load-management rather than as a separate framing mode, the 62% terminal cohort designed for as primary (transfer cohort moved to a second-course CS1.5/honors bridge), and the 73%/75% completion target reframed as a pipeline problem the curriculum cannot solve, paired with three named structural interventions (corequisite math, embedded-tutor advising touchpoints, non-submission intervention triggers). This is a replaced approach, not a polish.
what_surfaced: Challenger surfaced five concrete, citation-grounded gaps the opening pass missed: (1) the week-4 generative milestone falls past the Tinto first-4-6-week integration window and Wlodkowski/Ginsberg + Rendón validation windows, and trace-and-predict is receptive not generative; (2) the 12-cycle weekly handoff between three modes is exactly the redesign profile that degrades across a contingent/adjunct faculty pool (Eagan & Jaeger; Bailey Ch.5; CCCSE variance); (3) for the 62% terminal majority, Bailey's majority-population principle argues for terminal-cohort-primary design, not transfer-primary; (4) validation/belonging is at least as load-bearing as cognitive load for first-gen/ESL students, and trace-and-predict is a high-stereotype-threat assessment surface (Margolis/Fisher, Cheryan, Steele/Aronson); (5) the 73%/75% target is a pipeline/structural problem (advising, aid, prerequisite math) that course-level redesign reliably fails to move at open-admission colleges.
strongest_quote: PAS is cognitive-load-defensible but andragogy-suboptimal. Five concrete gaps require Round 2 revision: week-2/3 generative milestone, simpler architecture for adjunct pool, design for the 62% terminal cohort as primary, validation/belonging beyond cognitive-load, and pipeline framing followed all the way.

topic: TerraClarity employee-handbook organizational vocabulary: "team members" vs "employees" vs "colleagues"
what_changed: The core vocabulary verdict ("team members") survived unchanged, but the Challenger (employment-law attorney) materially reshaped the deliverable's implementation architecture. The Generator's opening front-matter clause clarified only terminology equivalence; the Challenger supplied a drafted alternative integrating equivalence + at-will + non-modification, which the Generator then accepted verbatim. The Challenger also forced previously-deferred NLRA Section 7 savings-clause architecture into the recommendation as required (not optional) structure, and forced the vague "narrow hybrid" into an enumerated boundary of statutory sections. The converged recommendation thus expanded in scope from a vocabulary pick to a vocabulary pick plus legally-binding implementation architecture.
what_surfaced: Three specific legal risks the management-science Generator missed: (1) the front-matter clause as drafted did not foreclose the Toussaint/Woolley-line implied-contract-from-handbook reading (a litigated risk), only terminology equivalence; (2) Stericycle (372 NLRB No. 113, 2023) and GC Memo 23-08 make collective-identity "team member" framing in confidentiality/civility/social-media/solicitation rules presumptively rights-chilling absent Section 7 savings clauses; (3) the unbounded "narrow hybrid" failed to enumerate which sections legally require "employee" register (FMLA notice, ADA accommodation, anti-harassment per Faragher/Ellerth, exempt-classification disclosures, IC-facing sections, and later I-9/USERRA/ERISA SPDs/OSHA/SOX-Dodd-Frank).
strongest_quote: The clarifying front-matter clause as drafted clarifies *terminology equivalence* but does not foreclose the *Toussaint*-line implied-promise reading; cited *Woolley* prominence standard, *Toussaint* "negation of apparent commitments" rule, and provided a drafted alternative that integrates equivalence + at-will + non-modification.
